ops.title: KeyCache EvictAll can wipe a concurrent different-endpoint Put
ops.description: Pre-existing race (present in the original code and every design tried during the v0.10.4 auth_status work; surfaced in the round-5 gpt-5.6-sol review, out of scope for that fix). In internal/cache/cache.go, KeyCache.heal calls `c.EvictAll()` after healingWrapper.heal returns healed=true, but a concurrent Put for a DIFFERENT endpoint that queued behind the heal can acquire w.mu, see the newly installed Enclave wrapper (healed=false for it), wrap+store its entry, and then have the healing Put's not-yet-run EvictAll wipe that correctly-Enclave-wrapped entry — with no error returned to either caller. Impact: a silent cache miss forcing one redundant re-prime (one extra Touch ID) for that endpoint; not data loss (keys are re-derivable). A proper fix makes the swap and eviction atomic w.r.t. entry publication (hold c.mu across both, or version the wrapper stored in entries so a stale-wrapper entry is refused rather than served/wiped). Anchor: internal/cache/cache.go KeyCache.heal (the `if healed { c.EvictAll() }` block).

ops.text: a heal's EvictAll cannot wipe an entry a concurrent different-endpoint Put stored with the post-swap wrapper
